displayText.simplePara: AMPEL360e Wide Tube-and-Wing Family (eWTW) — All variants

remarks.simplePara: Programme-controlled placeholder. infoCode 040A subject to BREX/SNS/DMRL freeze. Not for operational use.

levelledPara.title: General
levelledPara.para: This data module describes the Temperature Control System of the AMPEL360e eWTW ECS, aligned to ATLAS node 020-060 and ATA 21-60. The temperature control system maintains the temperature in each cabin zone, the cockpit and equipment bays within defined comfort and operational limits, using closed-loop control of trim air valves, pack output and supplemental heating elements.

levelledPara.title: Temperature Control Architecture
levelledPara.para: The temperature control system uses a multi-zone control architecture with independent temperature targets for each zone:

listItem.para: Zone Temperature Controllers (ZTC) — one per temperature zone, with redundant sensors.
Pack discharge temperature control — adjusts pack output temperature as a function of zone demand.
Trim air valves — add hot trim air to individual zone ducts to trim temperature upward from pack base temperature.
Flight deck temperature control — independent circuit with pilot and co-pilot selector inputs.

levelledPara.title: Sensors and Inputs
levelledPara.para: The temperature control system uses the following sensor types:

listItem.para: Cabin air temperature sensors (supply and return air, dual redundant per zone).
Crew-set temperature targets (cabin management panel and flight deck selectors).

levelledPara.title: Duct Overheat Protection
levelledPara.para: The temperature control system includes duct overheat detection and protection. If duct temperature exceeds a defined threshold, the system automatically reduces hot trim air supply and generates a crew advisory. Persistent overheat causes automatic closure of the affected trim air valve and generation of a maintenance message.
